item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: comfort zone，so i upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its really cool,but I don't know why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, didn't seem sarcastic to me. Glad you got it though, I was worried I've been wording my criticism badly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. I was never looking for this article to be the thing to introduce people to Fifthism/GAW to (I don't even think this article is in the top 15 highest-rated GAW pieces). Nonetheless, thank you for your critique; it'll help me figure out how to take certain approaches to short-form writing in the future. (I hope this sounds genuine and not snarky; I always find critique to be helpful even if I have no plans to implement it, and anyone taking their time to comment with constructive criticism are always welcome.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The overtness of your nods isn't going to matter to someone unfamiliar with Fifthism. If your intention is to write the article solely for people who are already going to be read-up on these groups then fair do, but without that knowledge like 80-85% of the oomph is taken out, and there isn't enough detail to lead people to start looking in to Fifthism or GAW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If there were required reading, I would have linked to it in the article. There isn't, so I link to nothing. (I mean, it's not like I could get any more overt to the Fifthist nods.) I don't write articles on the assumption that it'll be the first article a reader new to the site will find; that limits my writing in a way I don't enjoy. I would rather make something I find personally good and utterly incomprehensible to a newbie than something I don't feel proud of and have to water down to make it accessible, unless doing so is absolutely warranted. In this case, I don't feel it's absolutely warranted.

Understandable if you were meh about the execution. Thank you for reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeeeh, the article leans on required reading that it doesn't point the reader to, especially concerning information about the artist and the connection to Fifthism. It feels like it would be very inaccessible to somebody new to the site. All they would have to go on is the environmentalism bent and even that's kinda "meh" in its execution.
Let's protect the environment!

item.title: Re: How is this fifthist?
item.content:encoded: The reference to stars, the sky, number patterns and occurrences like the numbers 5 and 6, and smoke (by way of fire). This is, in a sense, a more positive/optimistic Fifthism.

item.title: How is this fifthist?
item.content:encoded: Can you explain why this is fifthist? I don't really know a lot about that GOI, to be honest, so I'd probably prefer if you linked some SCPs that would help me figure it out for myself. But I definitely liked it as a standalone article; especially with the apocalyptic undertones despite the environmentalist effect, with the plants and animals overtaking the earth. I like that kind of contrast in SCPs that makes things usually taken as positive appear in a more negative light, just to make people think and make the SCP more otherworldly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the imagery in this one.

Additionally, as an artistic SCP this one doesn't have the contrived feeling that so many other articles end up with. No need for excessive dadaist visuals or teaching a lesson through senseless violence or killing.

I agree with the above statement that this piece and lily's proposal coming out at the same time is fairly humorous timing given the nature of both anomalies.

Well done. +1.

item.title: Translations and Readings
item.content:encoded:
To promote translations and readings of my work and thank all the authors for their hard work, I am linking to them here in addition to my author page.

Translations

  • Chinese by Hannah_AI
  • Japanese by ukarayakara
  • Polish by Khnyber
  • Portuguese by cauazinlindaum

Readings

  • Doctor Cimmerian
  • Purgatoris
  • SCP情報局

Thank you all!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The imagery is surreal, intriguing and creative, but it ultimately feels a little…thin? Overly simplistic? Holding off on voting until I figure this out. Most likely going for the novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like it has some connection to lily-s-proposal which adds a nice buff to the intrigue behind it.

Good work.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also: I could excuse the Foundation not making a direct connection to the Fifthists in the article if it was longer. But when so many of the very limited details point to Fives, the Foundation probably oughta notice. Five earths, .5 mm, 5AM, 53 minutes of manifestation… they didn't notice any of that and at least deem it worthy of investigation or mention?

It’s irrelevant to put in my article because it’s info the reader can already infer for themselves, the Foundation doesn’t always point out or state the 5 (theres also some 6) numberology at play in things and even if they knew, its added fluff to my article that doesn’t cleanly fit anywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: C A N is CommunismAnarchismNihilism, the GAW member that made it. The Foundation doesn't seem to overtly notice the preponderance of fives in most Fifthist articles; and the skip is basically saying, 'hey, world is kinda ending, guys. Maybe help out a little, go green, and eldritch abominations and global warming won't gangbang us into extinction.'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess I really am dyslexic. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Doctor Wondertainment's Inflatable Invasion"?

I don't honestly get this one, and seeing the explanation does nothing for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Had to downvote this, reasoning below:

+ show block
– hide block

I feel like I'm missing something here. At first I thought it was some other article that'd contextualize this but Deci says this stands on its own so I maybe I'm missing something in the article itself. I'm willing to accept that maybe it's me if someone can clue me in on what I'm not getting.

I don't, for instance, understand why the last line has a strong impact on people. There's no aha moment there, since one can tell by the middle of the article that the thing has an environmental bent. The conclusion just seems like a natural direction to take it with no real subversion of expectations (and even if it was subverting the expectations that you set up one sentence prior; that's a weak execution).

There's also a lot here that just goes unexplained, and not in a "the author leaves it to your imagination" but rather "why isn't there more information in this file, in universe?"

What's the significance of "C A N"?
What's the significance of Ashworth Moor Reservoir?
What can be gleaned from the details available on the exaggerated earths?

Also: I could excuse the Foundation not making a direct connection to the Fifthists in the article if it was longer. But when so many of the very limited details point to Fives, the Foundation probably oughta notice. Five earths, .5 mm, 5AM, 53 minutes of manifestation… they didn't notice any of that and at least deem it worthy of investigation or mention?

Summary and TL:DR:

What's here is impeccably written and has incredibly potential, but doesn't capitalize on that potential. I don't need every article to be a 3,000+ worder, but if you're going for something this short it may behoove you to avoid including so many unfired guns. By the end I was just left unsatisfied with the article, and that's not the reaction I imagine I was supposed to have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed! Thank you so much for translating!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have translated this article into Chinese.

Beautiful article and good skip, and I upvote this because of the last line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GaW strikes again.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beautiful use of imagery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was created by GAW member CommunismAnarchismNihilism which is referenced by both the title of the skip (also referring to what the skip visualizes) as well as the artist callsign.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted it because i like the small changes you made from the draft I saw, but why is this tagged "gamers-against-weed"? Is it just invisible backstory? (I'm assuming the Fifthist tag is because of the "star-shaped objects".)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That last line made everything click in place very well.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For some reason I have a weakness for environmentally friendly skips. Maybe it’s because where I grew up they promoted recycling and public transportation like there’s no tomorrow.

+1 for mist awareness!

item.title: Author's Post: "Let me feel the moon"
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to the following:

  • The Great Hippo for grammar, wording, clinical tone and phrasing fixes, as well as article suggestions.
  • A Random Day for clinical tone fixes.
  • DolphinSlugchugger for grammar and general feedback.
  • thedeadlymoose because even though I implemented none of your changes, it still inspired me in which direction to go in a bit better.

If I forgot anyone, lemme know.

I wrote the draft to this after getting high and envisioning seeing the anomaly described here in-person. I felt compelled to write it almost immediately, and then I cleaned it up afterwards over the next twelve days.

GoI tags are accurate; don't change or remove.
